summary: American Regionalist painter and muralist. Mentored Jackson Pollock. Known for America Today murals, Achelous and Hercules, July Hay, and Missouri State Capitol mural. Shares name with his grand-uncle, a Missouri senator. Called his method 'the bump and the hollow.'

works.name: America Today (1930–1931)
works.description: Mural panels including Outreaching Hands and Instruments of Power, depicting American life in the 1920s.

recursive_suggestions: American Regionalism — cross-topic with Grant Wood
comprehensive_summary: Thomas Hart Benton was an American Regionalist painter who mentored Jackson Pollock. He called his method 'the bump and the hollow.' His America Today mural includes panels like Outreaching Hands and Instruments of Power depicting 1920s American life. He painted Achelous and Hercules (man struggles with bull), July Hay (scythes at Martha's Vineyard), and murals for the Missouri State Capitol. He shares his name with his grand-uncle, a Missouri senator nicknamed 'Old Bullion.' Benton said of Pollock that the only thing he learned was 'how to drink a fifth a day,' while Pollock said Benton 'came face to face with Michelangelo, and lost.'
